name: Philodendron billietiae 'Variegated'
aboutText: Philodendron billietiae 'Variegated' is a stunning variegated form of the beloved billietiae species. Known for its long-stalked, elongated heart-shaped leaves, this cultivar displays striking yellow to cream variegation patterns that contrast beautifully with its rich green base. The bright orange petioles add an additional layer of visual interest, making it a highly sought-after specimen among aroid collectors. Native to the tropical regions of French Guiana and Brazil, this climbing philodendron rewards patient growers with increasingly dramatic foliage as it matures.

quickFacts.light: Bright, indirect filter — higher light for variegation

morphology.variegation: Yellow to cream sectoral and marbled variegation

fieldNotes.title: Orange Petioles in Splashed Gold
fieldNotes.author: Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Philodendron billietiae was discovered in 1981 in French Guiana by Frieda Billiet. It is immediately recognizable by its long, strap-like leaves and bright orange petioles. The variegated clone, featuring bold splashes of yellow and cream, is one of the most stunning sights in aroid cultivation. A 1995 study on neotropical hemi-epiphytes highlighted billietiae's climbing habit, utilizing its orange petioles to position leaves outward to catch light. The variegated sections disrupt the photosynthesis rate, making it slower than the green form, but the combination of orange, green, and gold is unmatched.
